summarization.a: The line chart shows the F density (Atoms/Å³) at different heights (Å) for an initial sample and after 200 and 400 Ar⁺ impacts at 20 eV.
summarization.b: The line chart shows the F density (Atoms/Å³) at different heights (Å) for an initial sample and after 200 and 400 Ar⁺ impacts at 50 eV

b.question: How does the F distribution differ between 20 and 50 eV Ar⁺ bombardment?
b.answer: At 20 eV, F atoms are removed from the surface but the distribution remains narrow and localized. At 50 eV, higher energy ions push F atoms deeper into the SiO₂ bulk, causing the profile to broaden significantly. This buried F becomes less accessible for reactive ion etching.

b.question: What is the approximate F profile width at 50 eV after 400 Ar⁺ impacts?
b.answer: About 15 Å (spanning from h ≈ 10 to h ≈ 25 Å).

a.question: Why is F being pushed into the substrate problematic for layer-by-layer etching?
a.answer: Buried F becomes inaccessible to incoming Ar⁺ ions and cannot participate in reactive etching of Si.

a.question: Which Ar⁺ energy better maintains F at the surface for efficient etching?
